applicable_conditions: Bonvoy member
Member number attached to reservation
Property unable to honor confirmed reservation
compensation_or_rights: Nearby hotel accommodation
Potential cash compensation depending on brand and status
Potential points compensation depending on brand and status
summary: Marriott describes an Ultimate Reservation Guarantee for situations where a participating hotel cannot honor a reservation. Compensation depends on brand and member status.

applicable_conditions: US airline disruption
Controllable delay or cancellation
Airline commitment listed on DOT dashboard
compensation_or_rights: Rebooking
Meal voucher
Hotel accommodation
Ground transportation
Travel credit or miles in some cases
summary: The DOT dashboard summarizes airline commitments for controllable cancellations and delays, such as meals, hotels, transportation, and rebooking.

applicable_conditions: Oversold flight departing from a US airport
Passenger has a confirmed reservation and meets check-in and gate deadlines
Voluntary and involuntary denied boarding must be distinguished
Compensation eligibility depends on the reason for denial and alternate arrival time
compensation_or_rights: Airline must seek volunteers before involuntarily denying boarding for oversales
Volunteers may negotiate and must be told material voucher restrictions
Involuntarily bumped passengers must receive a written statement of rights
Eligible involuntarily bumped passengers may receive denied boarding compensation
Confirmed alternate transportation
summary: US DOT guidance distinguishes voluntary offers from involuntary denied boarding caused by oversales. Eligibility and compensation depend on the passenger meeting applicable deadlines, the reason for denial, and the delay in reaching the destination.

applicable_conditions: Flight departing from an airport in an EU Member State
Or flight arriving from a non-EU country when operated by a qualifying Community carrier, subject to Article 3 conditions
Confirmed reservation and other scope requirements under Article 3
Cancellation or long delay under the applicable provisions
compensation_or_rights: Article 8 reimbursement or rerouting under applicable conditions
Article 9 care under applicable conditions
Potential compensation under Articles 5 and 7, subject to eligibility requirements and defenses
summary: The primary EU legal text establishes minimum passenger rights for denied boarding, cancellation, and long delay. Route scope, timing, notice, operating carrier, and extraordinary circumstances can change which remedies apply.

applicable_conditions: Flight departing from a UK airport
Or flight arriving in the UK on a qualifying UK or EU operating carrier
Confirmed reservation and applicable check-in requirements
Fixed compensation can depend on arrival delay, cancellation notice, rerouting, and extraordinary circumstances
compensation_or_rights: Care during qualifying delays, including reasonable meals and overnight accommodation
Refund or rerouting under applicable conditions
Potential fixed compensation subject to route, timing, and extraordinary-circumstance rules
Written rights and remedies for qualifying involuntary denied boarding
summary: UK261 provides route-dependent rights for qualifying delays, cancellations, and denied boarding. Care, refund or rerouting, and fixed compensation have different triggers and defenses.

applicable_conditions: Flight to, from, or within Canada, including a connecting flight
Rights depend on whether the disruption was within the carrier's control, required for safety, or outside its control
Assistance and compensation have separate timing and eligibility requirements
Some duties and compensation rules vary between large and small carriers
compensation_or_rights: Reason information and periodic status updates
Rebooking or refund under applicable conditions
Food, communication, and overnight accommodation for qualifying disruptions
Potential inconvenience compensation for qualifying disruptions within the carrier's control
Denied-boarding protections
summary: Canada's APPR applies to flights to, from, and within Canada. The available assistance, rebooking, refund, or compensation depends on cause, timing, arrival delay, and carrier size.

policy_name: Refunds and Other Consumer Protections Final Rule
applicable_conditions: Scheduled flight to, from, or within the United States
The airline cancels or significantly changes the flight
The passenger does not accept the alternative transportation, credit, voucher, or other compensation offered
Refund timing and method requirements apply when a refund is owed
compensation_or_rights: Automatic refund when the rule's eligibility conditions are met
Refund to the original form of payment unless the passenger affirmatively chooses an alternative
Refund of the unused ticket amount and applicable fees
Notice of the right to a refund before an alternative is offered
summary: US DOT rules require prompt automatic refunds for qualifying cancellations or significant changes on flights to, from, or within the United States when the passenger does not accept an alternative. This is separate from airline meal and hotel commitments.

policy_name: Australian Consumer Law: Travel Delays and Cancellations
applicable_conditions: Travel service within Australia or an international travel service departing Australia
An inbound service may be covered when booked through a travel provider's Australian website
Whether service was provided within a reasonable time depends on delay length, cause, and the replacement offered
Third-party events outside the provider's control can change whether consumer guarantees supply a remedy
compensation_or_rights: Replacement travel service or refund when the consumer guarantee is not met
Potential reimbursement for reasonable replacement travel in some circumstances
Airline compensation policies cannot remove applicable consumer-guarantee rights
summary: Australian Consumer Law uses a context-specific reasonable-time standard rather than an EU-style fixed compensation table. A replacement, refund, or some consequential reimbursement may apply depending on the circumstances.

policy_name: Provisions on the Management of Flight Regularity (2016 Order No. 56)
applicable_conditions: Chinese carrier, or a foreign or Hong Kong, Macao, or Taiwan carrier whose flight originates or stops in mainland China
The carrier must publish its delay and cancellation service terms
Meal and accommodation duties at the origin depend on whether the cause is attributable to the carrier
No uniform national fixed delay-compensation amount is created by this regulation
compensation_or_rights: Delay or cancellation information and a written disruption certificate on request
Refund or ticket change according to applicable conditions
Meals or accommodation for carrier-caused delay or cancellation at the origin
Meals or accommodation for domestic-flight delay at a stopover or diversion point regardless of cause
On-board water and food after a qualifying tarmac delay
summary: China's flight-regularity regulation sets information, refund or change, care, and complaint duties. At the origin, carrier-caused disruptions receive stronger meal and accommodation duties than non-carrier causes.

policy_name: Public Air Transport Passenger Service Provisions (2021 Order No. 3)
applicable_conditions: Chinese carrier, or a foreign or Hong Kong, Macao, or Taiwan carrier whose flight originates or stops in mainland China
A carrier-caused involuntary change must be handled without a ticket-change fee when the regulatory conditions are met
An involuntary refund must not include a refund fee
Oversales rules require a volunteer process before boarding-priority rules are used
compensation_or_rights: Fee-free qualifying involuntary ticket change or transfer
No refund fee for an involuntary refund
Assistance completing a qualifying connecting itinerary
Oversales volunteer process, documented carrier standards, and compensation or service under those standards
Written proof of voluntary surrender or denied boarding caused by oversales
summary: China's passenger-service regulation covers involuntary ticket changes and refunds, connecting itineraries, and oversales procedures. It requires carriers to publish their denied-boarding compensation and service standards rather than imposing one national fixed amount.
